alsoKnownAs: Lesser Siren (Siren intermedia, sister species sold under the same trade name)
Harlequin Sulawesi Shrimp (older trade name; properly belongs to C. woltereckae)
Harlequin Shrimp (FW — distinct from saltwater Hymenocera picta)
Sulawesi Tiger Shrimp (older trade name; the 'Tiger' label is also applied to C. spinata in some sources)
Halloween Hermit (locally — not the Hawaiian Ciliopagurus strigatus)
Giant Gourami (erroneous — distinct from Osphronemus goramy)
Hong Kong Plec (misnomer — not a plecostomus)
NOT Siamese Algae Eater (Crossocheilus oblongus) — single clean stripe reaching tail tip, clear fins, no yellow
NOT False Siamese Algae Eater / Cambodian Log Sucker (Garra cambodgiensis) — lacks yellow dorsal band

entries.scientificName: Hymenochirus boettgeri — AMPHIBIAN (order Anura, family Pipidae), not a fish, but commonly kept in freshwater aquariums; filed under taxon=fish as a pragmatic concession pending schema amphibian support

alsoKnownAs: Fundulopanchax sjoestedti (spelling variant — sjoestedti/sjostedti orthographic only; accepted spelling per Catalog of Fishes is sjostedti)

NOTE — trade-name confusion: 'Bumblebee Catfish' is also applied to the Asian Bumblebee Catfish (Pseudomystus siamensis, fw-fish-144), a larger, more territorial Bagridae species reaching 15–20 cm. M. iheringi (SA) stays 6–8 cm and is far more peaceful. Always verify family (Pseudopimelodidae = SA; Bagridae = Asian) before purchase.
Ahli’s Hap (incorrect — refers to the separate species Sciaenochromis ahli)
Symphysodon discus (traditional Heckel — see taxonomic note)
Red Devil Cichlid (trade overlap with A. labiatus)

entries.scientificName: Synodontis petricola — CAUTION: virtually all fish sold under this name in the aquarium trade are actually S. lucipinnis (Dwarf Petricola, fw-fish-327), a smaller species formally described in 2006. True S. petricola reaches ~14 cm and is rarely exported; confirm ID before purchase.
alsoKnownAs: Cuckoo Catfish (shared trade name — also misapplied to S. multipunctatus)
Half-Banded Spiny Eel (trade misapplication — correctly M. circumcinctus)
Island Apple Snail (P. maculata trade name)
Anubias barteri var. nana × A. gilletii
Vesicularia dubyana (trade name used by some retailers; modern consensus is V. montagnei)
Susswassertang (incorrectly; Susswassertang is a different plant)
Peacock Moss (trade overlap; some retailers treat Peacock and Spiky as the same Taxiphyllum cultivar, others as distinct)
